description:
The epic assumed the type-expr grammar could stay shared and only name-resolution come in-house. Investigation shows the old-world parse is the WRONG SHAPE: parse_type_shape_with_vars (type_expr/parser.rs) welds a ModuleTypeEnv INTO the grammar -- it consults env.get_alias to decide name-vs-var and to validate arity at PARSE time (parser.rs:650,693) and interns type vars while parsing. That env is exactly the per-call, whole-program-assuming structure the northstar rejects, fused into the parser. compiler2 cannot reuse it without dragging the env back in. So the parse comes in-house too.

Principle: a NAME in a type is any reference bound into the NAMESPACE as a type at the point the spec/@type is declared.

1. PARSE is env-free and classification-free. Tokens -> a structural type-expr where names are bare Named{path,args}; NO parse-time var classification, NO arity validation, NO env. Pure grammar, compiler2-owned.

2. CAPTURE the namespace at the declaration point. The namespace is a hash-consed immutable BindingId -- capturing what is in scope here costs one u32, stored with the AST. A function already captures its scope (FunctionDef.namespace, identity.rs:208), so its @spec resolves against that. Each @type captures the scope at its declaration point alongside its held body AST. @type names are reserved in the FIRST define_scope walk so sibling/forward type references are in scope -- the namespace replaces the build_module_type_env fixpoint outright.

3. RESOLVE later (.3/.4) classifies each name against the captured namespace: a NamespaceSymbol::Type binding -> a named TypeName ref (-> TypeDefined -> Ty); an unbound lowercase name -> a free type var; a builtin -> a scalar. The env is gone; the namespace IS the resolution context.

Resolution still emits crate::specs::ResolvedSpec (params: Ty + param_shapes: ResolvedTypeShape), so specs::select scheme-matching is untouched. Boundary refines: in-house = parse + name-resolution up to producing Ty; shared = the specs scheme engine and algebra over resolved shapes/Ty. Legacy type_expr (parse_type_expr, ModuleTypeEnv, build_module_type_env) stays frozen for the dying fz frontend and is removed with it in fz-rh2.9.

Design decision to settle in implementation: whether the in-house structural type-expr is a new small syntactic enum (names + structure) resolved to ResolvedSpec, or a reuse of crate::specs::ResolvedTypeShape with var-classification deferred to resolution. Recommend a NEW in-house syntactic TypeExpr -- it keeps the syntactic (names) and resolved (vars/Ty) representations cleanly separate and avoids overloading ResolvedTypeShape::Var with a parse-time meaning it must not carry.

Unblocks .1 (reference walk = walk the in-house type-expr against the captured namespace) and .3 (resolver = namespace + TypeDefined walk producing ResolvedSpec).

Verify: in-house parse over the worked examples (foo(SomeModule.t(float), integer); a plain alias; a refines brand; (a,b)->{a,b} with free vars) yields the expected structure with names left unresolved and the captured namespace recorded; no ModuleTypeEnv is constructed anywhere on the compiler2 path.

comments.text: DECISION (settled): new in-house syntactic TypeExpr enum. Names stay bare Named{path,args}; structure via Tuple/List/Union/Arrow/Resource/StructRecord; NO Var, NO env at parse. resolve(TypeExpr, captured_namespace, &facts) -> crate::specs::ResolvedSpec (Ty + ResolvedTypeShape): a name bound as Type -> TypeName -> TypeDefined -> Ty; an unbound lowercase name -> free Var; a builtin -> scalar. Keeps syntactic and resolved layers separate; specs stays an output, not a dependency.

Commit boundary: .11 lands the env-free parser (TypeExpr/TypeDefBody + parser + 14 tests). The verify-line property "no ModuleTypeEnv on the compiler2 path" is the epic END-STATE, not provable here -- function_type_env still exists until .4 deletes it, and .7 guards it. Namespace CAPTURE wiring lands in .1 (functions already capture via FunctionDef.namespace; @types capture when reserved in the first define_scope walk).

title: In-house type-expr parse + namespace capture (retire the env-coupled shape parser for compiler2)
